school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: The class in Period 1's grade is exactly 15% more than Ellis' grade.
Studio goes with at least two of Dunn, Chemistry, 84%, and Period 5.
Nash's period is between the class with 74%'s period and the History class' period.
Frost, 79%, and Period 5 go with English, French, and History, in some order.
Period 4 goes with none of French, History, or Pavilion.
Either Pavilion goes with English or Nash goes with Library.
Either History goes with Workshop, or Music goes with Period 2 — but not both.
Library goes with exactly two of Ellis, Music, 84%, and Period 2.
The class in Period 1's grade is at least 7% more than the History class' grade.
The class in the Workshop's grade is higher than both Frost's grade and the class in Period 2's grade.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: The player who placed 5th's rating is exactly 300 more than Boris' rating.
Zugzwang goes with at least two of Anatoly, Benoni, 2085, and 1st.
Vera's placing is between the 1885-rated player's placing and the London player's placing.
Garry, 1985, and 1st go with French, Italian, and London, in some order.
2nd goes with none of Italian, London, or Skewer.
Either Skewer goes with French or Vera goes with Gambit.
Either London goes with Zwischenzug, or Pirc goes with 4th — but not both.
Gambit goes with exactly two of Boris, Pirc, 2085, and 4th.
The player who placed 5th's rating is at least 200 more than the London player's rating.
The player who loves the Zwischenzug's rating is higher than both Garry's rating and the player who placed 4th's rating.
